---
format:
  revealjs:
    output-file: bnr_cvd_incidence_2023_v1_slides.html
    theme: simple
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.06
    slide-level: 2
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: fade
    controls: true
    progress: true
    center: false
    logo: ../../../../assets/images/uwi-crestonly-20p.png
    footer: "Barbados National Registry | The University of the West Indies"
    css:
      - ../../../../assets/css/reveal-fonts.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
          :root {
            --bnr-navy: #001F3D;
            --bnr-teal: #045174;
            --bnr-sand: #D89C60;
            --bnr-orange: #E87A00;
            --bnr-grey: #5f6368;
            --stroke-purple: rgb(139, 111, 180);
            --heart-red: rgb(164, 22, 26);
          }

          .reveal {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            color: #222;
          }

          .reveal h1,
          .reveal h2,
          .reveal h3 {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            font-weight: 650;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
          }

          .reveal h1 { font-size: 1.95em; }
          .reveal h2 { font-size: 1.45em; margin-bottom: 0.55em; }
          .reveal h3 { font-size: 1.05em; }

          .reveal p,
          .reveal li {
            font-size: 0.78em;
            line-height: 1.25;
          }

          .title-wrap {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: 110px 1fr;
            gap: 28px;
            align-items: start;
            margin-top: 70px;
          }

          .title-wrap img {
            width: 96px;
            height: auto;
          }

          .title-heading {
            font-family: "BNR Sans", Arial, sans-serif;
            font-size: 1.95em;
            font-weight: 650;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            line-height: 1.1;
            margin: 0 0 0.35em 0;
          }

          .title-subtitle {
            font-size: 0.86em;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #222;
            margin-top: 0.15em;
          }

          .deck-kicker {
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-size: 0.62em;
            margin-top: 0.9em;
          }

          .deck-rule {
            width: 100%;
            height: 4px;
            background: var(--bnr-sand);
            margin-top: 28px;
          }

          .large-number {
            font-size: 2.2em;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: var(--bnr-navy);
            line-height: 1;
          }

          .label {
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-size: 0.56em;
            line-height: 1.15;
            margin-top: 0.25em;
          }

          .number-grid {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
            gap: 28px;
            margin: 34px 0 20px 0;
          }

          .number-card {
            border-top: 5px solid var(--bnr-sand);
            background: #f7f7f7;
            padding: 20px 18px 18px 18px;
            min-height: 130px;
          }

          .insight-box {
            background: #f7f7f7;
            border-left: 7px solid var(--bnr-sand);
            padding: 20px 24px;
            margin-top: 22px;
          }

          .muted { color: var(--bnr-grey); }

          .stroke {
            color: var(--stroke-purple);
            font-weight: 700;
          }

          .heart {
            color: var(--heart-red);
            font-weight: 700;
          }

          .figure-note {
            font-size: 0.56em;
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            margin-top: 0.4em;
          }

          .reveal .slide-logo {
            max-height: 54px !important;
          }

          .reveal section img {
            box-shadow: none;
            border: none;
          }

          .audience-citation {
            font-size: 0.82em;
            color: #222;
            line-height: 1.35;
            max-width: 1180px;
            margin-top: 0.8em;
          }

          .briefing-url-label {
            font-size: 0.72em;
            color: var(--bnr-grey);
            font-weight: 700;
            margin-top: 1.3em;
            margin-bottom: 0.25em;
          }

          .briefing-url {
            font-size: 0.82em;
            line-height: 1.25;
            color: var(--bnr-teal);
            font-weight: 700;
            word-break: break-word;
            max-width: 1180px;
          }

          .briefing-url a {
            color: var(--bnr-teal);
            text-decoration: underline;
            text-decoration-thickness: 0.08em;
            text-underline-offset: 0.12em;
          }
        </style>
---

::: {.title-wrap}
![](../../../../assets/images/uwi-crestonly-20p.png)

::: {}
<div class="title-heading">CVD Incidence in Barbados, 2010-2023</div>

<div class="title-subtitle">BNR CVD briefing, 2023</div>

::: {.deck-kicker}
Briefing created by the Barbados National Chronic Disease Registry, The University of the West Indies  
Group Contacts - Christina Howitt (BNR lead) - Ian Hambleton (analytics) - Updated on 7 Nov 2025
:::

::: {.deck-rule}
:::
:::
:::

## Key messages

Age-standardised incidence rates per 100,000 population, 2023.

::: {.number-grid}
::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">141</div>
<div class="label">stroke incidence among women</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">198</div>
<div class="label">stroke incidence among men</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">90</div>
<div class="label">heart attack incidence among women</div>
:::

::: {.number-card}
<div class="large-number">145</div>
<div class="label">heart attack incidence among men</div>
:::
:::

::: {.insight-box}
Incidence rates were higher among men for both stroke and heart attack.
:::

## Why this matters

Incidence rates show the rate at which new cardiovascular events are occurring in the population.

- Counts show pressure on services.
- Incidence rates allow fairer comparison over time.
- Including deaths before hospital care gives a fuller view of disease burden.

::: {.insight-box}
For heart attacks, hospital data alone can miss a substantial part of the burden.
:::

## What we did

We estimated annual age-standardised incidence rates for stroke and heart attack in Barbados from 2010 to 2023.

- We examined hospital-registered events.
- We added death-certificate-only events to estimate fuller population burden.
- We compared incidence patterns by event type, sex, and period.

## Annual <span class="stroke">stroke</span> and <span class="heart">heart attack</span> incidence rates, 2010-2023

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="68%"}
![](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_incidence_2023_v1/figures/cvd_incidence_annual.png){fig-alt="Annual age-standardised cardiovascular incidence rates from 2010 to 2023."}
:::

::: {.column width="32%"}
### Main pattern

- Stroke incidence was higher than heart attack incidence.
- Men had higher rates than women.
- Death-certificate-only events increased measured incidence, especially for heart attacks.

::: {.figure-note}
Rates are age-standardised per 100,000 population.
:::
:::
:::

## Interpretation

Hospital data and death certificates tell different parts of the incidence story.

Hospital data capture treated events. Death-certificate-only events capture people whose cardiovascular event was recorded as the underlying cause of death but who were not captured as hospital cases.

::: {.insight-box}
Combining both sources gives a more complete view of cardiovascular burden.
:::

## Incidence differences by event type, sex, and period

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="68%"}
![](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_incidence_2023_v1/figures/cvd_incidence_rate_ratios.png){fig-alt="Rate-ratio figure showing cardiovascular incidence differences by event type, sex, and period."}
:::

::: {.column width="32%"}
### Sex differences

- Men had higher incidence rates across the briefing outputs.
- The male excess was visible for both stroke and heart attack.
- These differences remain important for prevention and service planning.

::: {.figure-note}
Figure source: BNR incidence briefing outputs.
:::
:::
:::

## What this means

The incidence briefing shows continuing cardiovascular burden in Barbados.

The pattern is not only about hospital workload. It also reflects underlying disease risk, care-seeking, emergency response, and deaths occurring before hospital care.

::: {.insight-box}
Routine incidence monitoring helps turn registry data into planning intelligence.
:::

## Outputs and citation

Tables, figure data, metadata, workbook files, and build records are available in the online briefing.

::: {.audience-citation}
Barbados National Registry. *CVD Incidence in Barbados, 2010-2023: BNR CVD briefing, 2023*. Barbados National Chronic Disease Registry, The University of the West Indies. Accessed: [insert date accessed].
:::

::: {.briefing-url-label}
Online briefing
:::

::: {.briefing-url}
[https://uwi-bnr.github.io/info-hub/surveillance/cvd/briefings/case-incidence.html](https://uwi-bnr.github.io/info-hub/surveillance/cvd/briefings/case-incidence.html)
:::
